content: Curiosity-driven active learning agents with 2024–2025 state-of-the-art memory can be framed as self-improving autonomous agents that grow their memory footprint selectively by prioritizing high-uncertainty experiences, mirroring how biological agents prioritize memory encoding for unexpected events. This creates a new design paradigm where an agent’s memory architecture is itself actively updated by the agent’s own curiosity signal, rather than being pre-allocated by developers.
